page: Amazon Web Services c7i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, delivering 192 virtual CPUs across 96 physical cores with a base clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations. The system is configured with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c7i.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test and PassMark CPU score both rank in the top 10%, reflecting the high parallel execution capacity of its 192 vCPUs. Memory bandwidth benchmarks, including both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads and writes, also place in the top 10% tier. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with the stress-ng single-core CPU test scoring in the middle 50% tier. The instance also shows strong capabilities in specialized workloads, scoring in the top 10% for PassMark encryption, physics, and Geekbench ray tracing, while achieving top-tier results in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput.

From a resource density perspective, the c7i.48xlarge offers high compute density but presents a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, which may limit its efficiency for memory-bound applications. The lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, making it dependent on external network storage and unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or heavy local disk I/O. However, the inclusion of AMX instructions enables strong performance in CPU-based machine learning, as evidenced by top 25% scores in LLM inference prompt processing for 7B and 70B models. This hardware profile is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as high-performance web serving, multi-threaded CPU rendering, cryptography, and distributed compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C x86_64 processor with 192 vCPUs, 96 physical cores, and a 3.2 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, ranking in the top 10% in stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU benchmarks. It also supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions, yielding strong results in cryptography and CPU-based LLM inference. With a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps, the c7i.48xlarge is designed for highly parallel, compute-heavy workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and cryptography, though its low memory-to-CPU ratio and lack of local storage represent key architectural tradeoffs.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 192 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon processor, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for highly parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 192 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86 platform featuring 192 vCPUs and DDR5 memory for demanding parallel processing and cryptographic workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 192-vCPU Intel Xeon 8488C processor with a 3.2 GHz clock speed
Equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix math
Features 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs
Best suited for multi-threaded compilation, cryptography, and high-throughput web serving
